A 2 a.m. 'good morning' graphic sent straight to me.

I used to think being a mom was exhausting. Turns out, being 'my mom's kid' isn't a part-time gig either.
en.editorNote: Hits hardest for adult kids — share it with friends and watch the 'oh my god, same' replies roll in. Add 'happy Mother's Day, never change' for a soft landing
variations: Mom's retired and somehow busier than ever — mostly with worrying about me
The number of group chats my mom is in is a direct measure of her energy

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'sorry for every eye roll between 2008-2018' meme; rewritten as a span apology

zh-tw.content:
媽，這封是延遲十五年的道歉信：
我十二到二十五歲之間翻過的每一個白眼、頂過的每一句嘴、覺得妳很煩的每一個瞬間，
現在我全部回收，連同利息一起還給妳。

妳那時候每一句話，後來都被我活成了真的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節超殺催淚向，傳給媽媽她真的會哭。適合做成卡片正中間那一句，搭配一句「謝謝妳沒放棄我」
variations: 我青春期欠妳的白眼，現在用早安圖一張一張還
原來妳當年說的每一句煩人的話，後來都變成我的人生攻略

en.content:
Mom, this is a fifteen-year-late apology letter:
For every eye roll, every 'you don't get it,' every time I thought you were being annoying between the ages of twelve and twenty-five —
I'm taking it all back. With interest.

Everything you said back then? I ended up living it out, word for word.
en.editorNote: Heavy Mother's Day tearjerker — send this and mom will cry. Works as the centerpiece of a handwritten card with 'thanks for not giving up on me' underneath
variations: All those teenage eye rolls — I'm paying them back, one good morning text at a time
Everything you nagged me about turned out to be the user manual for my life

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'powered by love, caffeine, and mild confusion' caption; rewritten as bilingual mom-mode badge

zh-tw.content:
我媽的運轉模式：
百分之四十是愛、
百分之三十是擔心、
百分之二十是嘮叨、
剩下的百分之十——是「我這樣做還不是為你好」。

這個系統運行三十幾年沒當機，連客服都沒換過人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 百分比體文案，視覺好做圖、文字好分享。可以做成母親節限動梗圖，或印在T恤、馬克杯上送媽媽
variations: 我媽是內建愛、嘮叨和擔心的綜合機型
我家最穩的伺服器是我媽，三十年沒重開機過

en.content:
My mom runs on:
40% love,
30% worry,
20% nagging,
and 10% 'I'm only doing this for your own good.'

That system has been live for thirty-plus years with zero downtime — and the support line is always the same person.
en.editorNote: Percentage-style breakdown — perfect for a graphic, an Instagram story, or a printed mug for Mother's Day. Easy to remix with your own ratios
variations: Mom is the deluxe edition: love, worry, and nagging all built-in
The most reliable server in our house is my mom — thirty years uptime, no reboot

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'reheated coffee and unfinished sentences' mom life caption; rewritten as a daily routine punchline

zh-tw.content:
我媽的一天：
第一杯咖啡在微波爐裡放到涼，再加熱一次，又忘了。
她的話總是講到一半，因為突然想起冰箱還有一鍋滷肉要關火。
但只要是關於我的事，她可以一口氣講完整段不喘氣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節用，傳給媽媽會被她秒讀「對啦這就是我」。做成圖卡發限動轉發率超高
variations: 我媽的咖啡只有兩種狀態：剛泡好沒喝，跟微波第三次
我媽講話常常斷在一半，但講到我，從來沒漏過一句

en.content:
A day in my mom's life:
First cup of coffee goes cold in the microwave, gets reheated, and forgotten again.
Sentences end halfway because she suddenly remembers the stew on the stove.
But when it's about me, she can talk for ten minutes without pausing to breathe.
en.editorNote: Mother's Day post — your mom will read this and immediately say 'that's literally me.' Great for a shareable graphic or story
variations: Mom's coffee exists in two states: just brewed and untouched, or reheated for the third time
Mom's sentences trail off — except when it's about me, then it's a TED talk

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'know where everyone's shoes are' motherhood meme; rewritten as a household navigation joke

zh-tw.content:
在我家，找東西的標準流程只有一個：
第一步——自己找三分鐘。
第二步——放棄，大喊「媽——！」
第三步——媽從廚房說一句「就在你眼前那個櫃子第二層左邊」。

她連我房間都很少進，卻比 Google 地圖還準。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合家庭群組梗圖向，全家人都會點頭。母親節傳給媽媽會收到「就是這樣沒錯」的回覆
variations: 我媽是我家內建的搜尋引擎，輸入「我的東西呢」就有答案
在我家，最快的尋物方式不是翻箱倒櫃，是問媽

en.content:
In my house, the search algorithm has three steps:
1. Look for three minutes.
2. Give up. Yell 'MOM!'
3. From the kitchen: 'Second shelf, left side of the cabinet behind you.'

She barely goes into my room, but her accuracy beats Google Maps.
en.editorNote: Family group chat material — everyone nods at this one. Share with mom for a guaranteed 'yep, sounds about right'
variations: Mom is the built-in search engine of our house — query: 'where's my stuff,' result: instant
Fastest way to find anything at home isn't searching — it's asking mom

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'trauma just enough to be funny' sarcastic mom thank-you quote; rewritten as a tongue-in-cheek growth story

zh-tw.content:
媽，謝謝妳把我養成現在這個樣子——
會煩惱、會內耗、會半夜想人生，
但也會自己煮一頓飯、自己處理爛事、自己撐過難關。

剛剛好的崩潰，跟剛剛好的堅強，比例抓得很好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 反諷溫馨向，適合給懂的朋友看會大笑，傳給媽媽她會邊翻白眼邊笑。母親節卡片裡放這句很有層次
variations: 媽，妳養出來的我，能崩潰也能自己站起來，這配方剛剛好
謝謝妳給了我剛好夠用的脆弱跟剛好夠用的韌性

en.content:
Mom, thanks for raising me into this exact person —
the one who overthinks, spirals at 2 a.m., and questions every life choice,
but also cooks her own meals, fixes her own messes, and pulls herself through.

Just enough breakdown. Just enough backbone. You nailed the ratio.
en.editorNote: Sarcastic-sweet — friends will laugh, mom will roll her eyes and smile. Works beautifully inside a Mother's Day card with real heart
variations: Mom raised me to fall apart AND put myself back together — perfect recipe
Thanks for giving me just enough fragility and just enough grit

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'wet towels on the floor is a federal offense' joke; rewritten as a household law list

zh-tw.content:
我媽的愛無條件，但有幾條附加條款：
第一條，濕毛巾不能丟床上。
第二條，吃完飯的碗不能泡超過半小時。
第三條，外套不可以掛在椅背。
違反任何一條，愛還是在，但會附贈一段約三分鐘的演講。
zh-tw.editorNote: 家規體幽默，特別戳到還住家裡或常被念的人。可以做成「我媽家法十條」系列梗圖
variations: 我媽愛我無上限，但濕毛巾丟床上她可以氣三天
我家的愛是無條件的，附加條款佔了 A4 兩面

en.content:
My mom's love is unconditional — with a few clauses:
Clause 1: Wet towels do not belong on the bed.
Clause 2: Dirty dishes don't soak for more than thirty minutes.
Clause 3: Jackets are not chair decorations.
Violating any clause: love remains, but comes bundled with a three-minute lecture.
en.editorNote: House-rules humor — hits hardest for anyone still living at home or recently moved out. Great as a 'Mom's Ten Commandments' meme series
variations: Mom's love has no limit — but wet towels on the bed can ruin her week
The love is unconditional. The terms and conditions are two pages long.

items.sourceNote: inspired by emotional-funny short message angle; rewritten as a 'lessons unnoticed' Mother's Day reflection

zh-tw.content:
我以前以為媽媽教我的事，是「怎麼洗衣服、怎麼煮泡麵、怎麼搭公車」。
後來才發現，她真正教我的是：
怎麼在很累的時候還是好好吃飯、
怎麼在被誤會的時候不馬上爆炸、
怎麼在愛一個人的時候，不要忘了愛自己。

這些她從來沒明講，但每一天都活給我看。
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節溫情向長文，很適合做成 IG 圖文或卡片內頁文案。讀完會想立刻打給媽媽
variations: 我媽沒有用嘴教我，是用三十年活給我看
她教我的最重要的事，從來沒寫在功課本上

en.content:
I used to think the things mom taught me were 'how to do laundry, how to make instant noodles, how to take the bus.'
Turns out, what she really taught me was:
how to still eat properly when you're exhausted,
how to not explode the second you're misunderstood,
how to love someone without forgetting to love yourself.

She never said any of it out loud. She just lived it, every single day.
en.editorNote: Tender Mother's Day long-form — perfect for an Instagram carousel or the inside of a handwritten card. Reading it makes you want to call mom right now
variations: Mom didn't lecture me — she lived the lesson for thirty years straight
The most important things she taught me were never on a worksheet

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'silence is suspicious' parenting humor; rewritten for Chinese parenting context

zh-tw.content:
我媽說：「家裡太安靜的時候才最可怕。」
當年我不懂，現在我自己當媽才知道，
安靜不是和平，安靜是有人正在闖禍中。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合分享給有小孩的朋友，或是傳給媽媽當作「我終於懂了」的告白文。母親節傳給媽媽特別有共鳴
variations: 小時候我媽說家裡太安靜會出事，我以為她疑心病，現在我才知道她是預言家
安靜＝有事。這是我媽教我的第一條偵探守則

en.content:
My mom always said: "When the house gets too quiet, that's when you should be terrified."
I didn't get it as a kid.
Now I'm a parent and I get it — silence isn't peace, silence is evidence.
en.editorNote: Great share for parent friends or to send back to mom as a 'you were right all along' message. Mother's Day gold
variations: Mom said quiet houses meant trouble. I thought she was paranoid. Turns out she was a prophet
Silence equals crime scene. That's the first rule of detective work my mom taught me

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'human ATM' humor angle; reworked into a fridge-based metaphor

zh-tw.content:
我媽的冰箱永遠是滿的，但她永遠在說「沒東西可以吃」。
我家的櫃子永遠是亂的，但她永遠找得到我藏起來的零食。
這是一種我至今無法理解的物理現象。
zh-tw.editorNote: 親情系幽默，幾乎每個台灣家庭都會有共鳴。適合 IG 限動或群組裡丟出來大家會回「+1」
variations: 冰箱滿滿，媽媽說沒菜；櫃子亂亂，媽媽找得到我藏的零食。媽媽的世界自有邏輯
我媽能在三秒內找到我藏一年的零食，但找她自己的眼鏡要找半小時

en.content:
My mom's fridge is always full, but she's always saying "there's nothing to eat."
The kitchen cabinets are a disaster, but she can find the snacks I hid in 0.3 seconds.
Science cannot explain this.
en.editorNote: Family humor that lands in any group chat — universally relatable for anyone who's ever tried to sneak snacks past mom
variations: Full fridge, nothing to eat. Messy cabinets, instant snack detection. Mom logic is a closed system
Mom can locate a candy bar I hid a year ago in three seconds, but her glasses are missing for thirty minutes

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'CEO of crisis management' angle; reframed as a job description spoof

zh-tw.content:
如果媽媽的工作要寫履歷：
年資：永久。
薪水：沒有，但附情緒勞動。
專長：找東西、煮飯、心理諮商、危機處理、看穿謊言。
離職原因：無，且不接受辭呈。
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節職場風幽默，傳到家庭群組或公司群組都會被瘋傳。適合做成圖卡
variations: 媽媽這個職位：沒有試用期、沒有退休金、沒有辦法辭職，但她從來沒抱怨過待遇
全世界最難的工作叫做媽媽，沒有 KPI 但 24 小時 on call

en.content:
If "mom" were a job listing:
Tenure: permanent.
Salary: none, plus unlimited emotional labor.
Skills required: locating lost items, cooking, therapy, crisis management, lie detection.
Resignation: not accepted.
en.editorNote: Workplace-style Mother's Day humor — perfect for office group chats or as a shareable card design. Lands with working parents especially
variations: The mom position: no probation, no pension, no resignation accepted. And she's never once complained about the benefits
Hardest job in the world: mom. No KPIs, but you're on call 24/7

items.sourceNote: inspired by emotional Mother's Day short-message format; rewritten as a 'now I understand' realization

zh-tw.content:
小時候我覺得媽媽很煩，
長大後我才發現，那是全世界最便宜的關心，
而且只剩她願意免費提供。
媽，今年母親節，換我煩你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節最佳簡訊文案之一，前面好笑後面戳心。適合直接傳給媽媽，或是做成卡片內頁
variations: 以前覺得我媽很煩，現在覺得：原來那是市面上最划算的關心
媽，今年換我每天問你吃飽沒、冷不冷、累不累

en.content:
As a kid I thought mom was annoying.
As an adult I realized: that was the cheapest form of caring on the market,
and she's the only one still offering it for free.
Mom, this Mother's Day, it's my turn to annoy you.
en.editorNote: One of the best Mother's Day text messages — funny opener, gut-punch ending. Perfect to send directly or to letter inside a card
variations: Used to think mom was annoying. Turns out it was the best-value love in the market
Mom, this year it's my turn to ask if you ate, if you're cold, and if you got enough sleep

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'finder of lost things' humor; rewritten as a household search engine bit

zh-tw.content:
我家裡有一個搜尋引擎，比 Google 還準。
關鍵字輸入：「媽，我那個放哪？」
零點三秒回覆：「就在你眼前的櫃子第二格，你眼睛是裝飾的嗎？」
而且完全免費，還附贈情緒分析。
zh-tw.editorNote: 獻給所有找東西先喊媽的人，傳家庭群組保證共鳴。母親節時搭配「謝謝媽媽搜尋引擎」很可愛
variations: 我家搜尋引擎叫做媽，比 AI 還聰明，因為她連我自己都還沒想到的東西在哪都知道
找東西的三個步驟：一、找；二、找不到；三、喊媽。前兩步通常可以跳過

en.content:
There's a search engine in my house more accurate than Google.
Query: "Mom, where's my thing?"
Response in 0.3 seconds: "Second shelf of the cabinet right in front of you. Are your eyes for decoration?"
Completely free, with sentiment analysis included.
en.editorNote: For everyone who yells for mom before actually looking. Family group chat gold. Pair with a 'thanks, Mom Search Engine' line on Mother's Day
variations: Mom is the smartest AI in the house — she finds things I haven't even realized I'm missing yet
How to find something: 1) Look. 2) Don't find it. 3) Yell for mom. Steps 1 and 2 are optional

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'running out of patience' workout joke; reframed as a gym membership bit

zh-tw.content:
我媽不去健身房，但她每天都在做訓練。
項目包含：跑去找我、跳起來罵人、舉重買菜、深蹲撿東西、最後拉伸對我的耐心。
她說這套課程已經連續上了三十年，從來沒請過假。
zh-tw.editorNote: 媽媽日常的運動哏，適合母親節做成趣味圖文，也適合傳給愛健身的朋友當笑點
variations: 我媽的健身課表：早上罵人有氧、中午買菜重訓、晚上拉伸耐心。教練是我
媽媽不用辦健身卡，因為小孩就是她的全套器材

en.content:
My mom doesn't go to the gym, but she trains every day.
Daily routine: sprinting to check on me, jumping to yell, lifting groceries, squatting to pick things up, and stretching her patience.
She's been on this program for thirty years and never missed a day.
en.editorNote: A workout-themed mom joke — works well as a Mother's Day image post or shared with fitness friends. Lands with millennials especially
variations: Mom's gym schedule: morning cardio (yelling), afternoon weights (groceries), evening stretches (patience). I'm her trainer
Moms don't need a gym membership. They have kids — full body workout, lifetime contract

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'before you were here I loved you' classic; rewritten as a timeline reflection

zh-tw.content:
在我會走路之前，妳已經為我繞了無數圈。
在我會說話之前，妳已經懂我所有的哭。
在我懂事之前，妳就已經把最好的版本留給我。
而我，現在才剛開始學會說一句：謝謝妳，媽。
zh-tw.editorNote: 比較抒情的母親節文案，適合做卡片、IG 限動，或是不擅長表達的人直接複製傳給媽媽
variations: 我長大的速度，永遠追不上妳愛我的速度
在我學會感謝之前，妳已經給了我一輩子的理由

en.content:
Before I could walk, you'd already circled the world for me.
Before I could speak, you understood every cry.
Before I knew anything, you'd already saved your best for me.
And I'm only now learning to say: thank you, Mom.
en.editorNote: A softer, more lyrical Mother's Day piece — perfect for cards, Instagram stories, or anyone who struggles to put feelings into words. Just copy and send
variations: I'll never grow up fast enough to catch up with how much you've loved me
Before I learned the word 'thank you,' you'd already given me a lifetime of reasons to say it

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'free Wi-Fi mom' joke; rewritten as a household subscription service bit

zh-tw.content:
我家有一個月費 0 元的訂閱服務，叫做媽。
功能包含：無限熱食、24 小時心理諮商、衣物乾洗、生活提醒、情感支援。
沒有廣告、沒有試用期限、不會跳出來叫你升級。
唯一缺點是：等你想付費感謝她的時候，她說免錢。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把媽媽比喻成訂閱服務的科技哏，年輕族群最有共鳴。母親節傳到家人或同事群組超適合
variations: 全世界 CP 值最高的訂閱方案就是媽，可惜不能升級成「不會老的版本」
媽媽這個 APP 不用下載、不用註冊、不會卡，只是我們經常忘了打開

en.content:
There's a $0/month subscription service in my house called Mom.
Features: unlimited home cooking, 24/7 therapy, laundry service, life reminders, emotional support.
No ads, no free trial expiring, no pop-ups asking you to upgrade.
The only downside: when you finally want to pay her back, she says it's on the house.
en.editorNote: A tech-flavored mom joke that hits hard with younger crowds. Great for Mother's Day family or office group chats — easy to turn into a shareable graphic
variations: Best-value subscription in the world: Mom. Shame there's no 'never gets older' upgrade tier
The Mom app: no download, no signup, never crashes. We just forget to open it

items.sourceNote: inspired by the 'because I said so' mom logic trope; rewritten as a courtroom verdict bit

zh-tw.content:
我媽家裡的最高法院只有一條法條：
第一條：因為我這樣說。
第二條：請參照第一條。
上訴駁回，理由是「我是你媽」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 獻給每個從小聽「因為我說了算」長大的人，傳家庭群組必有人接話。也適合做圖文哏
variations: 我媽的話就是聖旨，我的反駁就是噪音
在我家，講道理永遠輸給「我吃過的鹽比你吃過的飯多」

en.content:
There's one law in my mom's house and it has two clauses:
Clause 1: Because I said so.
Clause 2: Refer to Clause 1.
Appeal denied. Grounds: I'm your mother.
en.editorNote: For everyone raised on 'because I said so.' Drop it in the family chat — somebody will reply with their own version. Also great as a meme
variations: Mom's word is law. My counterargument is noise
In our house, logic always loses to 'I've been alive longer than you'

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'wet towel on the floor is a federal offense' joke; reframed as a household criminal code

zh-tw.content:
我媽的愛是無條件的。
但有幾項例外：濕毛巾丟地上、襪子塞沙發縫、外套掛椅背、碗放在桌上不收。
以上每一項都是家庭重罪，量刑標準隨她當天心情調整。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給室友、伴侶或室友身分的伴侶，看完會默默把毛巾掛好。母親節時也是一種反向告白
variations: 在我家，亂丟襪子的刑期比作弊還重
媽媽的容忍度很高，但碰到濕毛巾跟地板接吻她就會破例

en.content:
Mom's love is unconditional.
With a few exceptions: wet towels on the floor, socks shoved between couch cushions, jackets draped on chairs, dishes left on the table.
Each one is a household felony. Sentencing depends on her mood that day.
en.editorNote: Send to roommates, partners, or partners who act like roommates. They will quietly hang up the towel. Sneaky way to thank Mom on Mother's Day too
variations: In my house, leaving socks out gets a longer sentence than cheating on a test
Mom tolerates a lot. But a wet towel kissing the floor? That's where she draws the line

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'running on coffee and mom strength' caption; rewritten as a fuel composition breakdown

zh-tw.content:
我媽的能源組成分析：
40% 咖啡、30% 對小孩的愛、20% 對小孩的氣、10% 不知道哪來的意志力。
科學家研究多年，仍無法複製這個配方。
目前已知唯一補給站，叫做「再撐一下」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 媽媽辛苦的真實寫照，母親節做成圖片或卡片內文都很有共鳴。也適合送給當媽的姊妹淘
variations: 媽媽的續航力來源：一杯咖啡、一句「沒事」，還有一個睡得很熟的小孩
別人靠睡眠回血，媽媽靠咖啡跟硬撐

en.content:
Breakdown of mom fuel:
40% coffee, 30% love for the kids, 20% irritation at the kids, 10% mystery willpower nobody can identify.
Scientists have studied the formula for years. Still unreplicable.
The only known recharge station is labeled "just five more minutes."
en.editorNote: An honest portrait of mom energy — works as a Mother's Day card or graphic. Also a great send for friends who just became moms
variations: Mom's battery life: one cup of coffee, one 'I'm fine,' and one kid who's finally asleep
Other people recharge with sleep. Moms recharge with caffeine and sheer force of will

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'mom is therapist, chef, free wifi' multi-role joke; rewritten as an over-the-top job posting

zh-tw.content:
媽媽這個職位的工作內容：
廚師、司機、心理師、會計、護理師、翻譯、保鑣、夜間客服、心情垃圾桶、人生導師。
月薪：零元。
年資：永久。
離職率：零，因為她從來沒想過離職。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把媽媽的多重身分濃縮成一個荒謬職缺，母親節傳給媽媽會被酸但她會偷笑。職場群組也很有共鳴
variations: 媽媽這份工作沒有合約、沒有獎金、沒有休假，但永遠不會被資遣
全天下最難的職位：媽媽。應徵條件超嚴苛，福利只有一句「我愛你」

en.content:
Job description: Mom.
Duties: chef, driver, therapist, accountant, nurse, translator, bodyguard, late-night customer support, emotional dumpster, life coach.
Salary: $0.
Contract: lifetime.
Turnover rate: 0%, because she never even considered quitting.
en.editorNote: Mom's many hats compressed into a ridiculous job listing. Send to mom on Mother's Day — she'll roll her eyes and laugh. Also lands hard in work group chats
variations: Mom: a job with no contract, no bonus, no PTO, and zero chance of being laid off
Hardest job in the world: Mom. Brutal requirements, only benefit is 'I love you'

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'nothing is truly lost until Mom can't find it' classic; rewritten with a household-search angle

zh-tw.content:
東西不見不算真的不見，要等到我媽找不到才算。
我找半小時叫粗心，她找三秒鐘叫奇蹟。
結論：我家的東西只認媽媽。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合貼在家庭群組、或是任何一個被媽媽嫌「眼睛長哪裡」的時刻發出來。共鳴爆表
variations: 我找不到的東西，我媽五秒鐘就從我面前那個明明很明顯的位置撿起來給我
媽媽不是找到東西，是東西看到她就乖乖出現

en.content:
Nothing is truly lost until Mom also can't find it.
I search for thirty minutes — that's called being careless.
She searches for three seconds — that's called a miracle.
Conclusion: stuff in this house only listens to her.
en.editorNote: Drop this in the family group chat — or any time mom has asked you 'where are your eyes?' Universal experience
variations: Mom doesn't find lost things. Lost things see her coming and surrender
Me looking for 20 minutes: nothing. Mom walks in, picks it up off the table I was standing next to

items.sourceNote: inspired by mom-as-Happy-Meal / mom-as-snack joke; rewritten with a vending machine framing

zh-tw.content:
當媽以後我才發現，我不是人，我是販賣機。
小孩按一下要點心、按兩下要抱抱、按三下要回答「為什麼天空是藍色的」。
而且我還不能投幣，是無限取用制。
zh-tw.editorNote: 新手媽媽群組轉發率超高，傳給剛當媽的朋友她會邊笑邊哭。也適合母親節幫媽媽出一口氣
variations: 媽媽其實是一台壞掉的販賣機：永遠不能拒絕，永遠出貨
原來「媽——」這個音，就是按下販賣機按鈕的聲音

en.content:
Becoming a mom, I realized I'm not a person — I'm a vending machine.
One button for snacks. Two buttons for hugs. Three buttons for 'why is the sky blue.'
And nobody puts coins in. It's just unlimited free dispensing forever.
en.editorNote: Send to any friend who just had a kid — they'll laugh-cry. Also a great Mother's Day caption for any mom in the trenches
variations: Mom = a broken vending machine. Can't say no, always restocked, never paid
Turns out 'MOOOM' is just the sound of someone pressing the vending machine button

items.sourceNote: inspired by viral 'no flowers at Walmart so mama getting cilantro' tweet; rewritten with a local 7-11 angle

zh-tw.content:
母親節，花店全部賣完。
我衝去 7-11，最後只剩一束蔥。
我媽收到的時候說：
「沒關係，這個我用得到，玫瑰我用不到。」
我突然覺得，她可能比我還適合過這個節日。
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節限定哏，臨時抱佛腳的孝子孝女最懂。配上一張蔥的照片轉發效果加倍
variations: 媽媽收到我送的蔥說：剛好今天要煮蛋花湯。母愛真的可以把任何東西轉成禮物
別人媽媽收花會哭，我媽收花會問：「這多少錢？貴吧？拿去退！」

en.content:
Mother's Day. Every flower shop was sold out.
I ran to the corner store. All they had left was a bunch of green onions.
When I gave them to mom, she said:
'It's fine — I can actually use these. Roses don't go in soup.'
And honestly? She's better at this holiday than I am.
en.editorNote: Last-minute Mother's Day energy. Pair with a photo of grocery store flowers (or actual produce) for max impact
variations: Mom unwrapped the onions and said 'perfect, I was making soup anyway.' Moms can turn anything into a gift
Other moms cry receiving flowers. My mom asked the price and told me to return them

items.sourceNote: inspired by Nora Ephron teenager/dog quote; rewritten as a teenager-era survival observation

zh-tw.content:
我媽說，等小孩進入青春期，家裡一定要養一隻狗。
因為這樣，至少還有一個生物，看到她回家會搖尾巴。
我聽完想反駁，但想想我國中那三年——
算了，狗值得有。
zh-tw.editorNote: 家裡有青少年的爸媽會狂點頭，傳給朋友圈裡剛升格成青少年爸媽的人正合適
variations: 我媽養狗的真正原因：青春期的我，連「嗯」都不想回她
青春期的小孩 vs 狗：一個關門摔得很大聲，一個搖尾巴搖斷

en.content:
My mom says once your kid hits the teen years, you absolutely need a dog.
That way, at least one living thing in the house is happy to see her come home.
I was going to argue. Then I remembered middle school me.
Yeah. Dog deserves it.
en.editorNote: Parents of teenagers will violently agree. Send to any friend whose kid just turned 13 — and brace for the LOL reply
variations: Real reason mom got a dog: teen me wouldn't even grunt back at her
Teenager vs. dog: one slams the door, one wags a tail. Mom knows which one she's investing in

items.sourceNote: inspired by classic mom-texts-abbreviations joke (IDK / LY / TTYL); rewritten as a LINE sticker era version

zh-tw.content:
我媽傳訊息來問：
「兒子，878 是什麼意思？」
我說：「就『掰七八』，掰掰的意思啦。」
她隔了三秒回我：
「喔好，那你以後不要再對我說這個。」
從那天起，我學會了好好說再見。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給平常都用貼圖跟媽媽對話的人，她一定有共鳴。也適合母親節提醒自己：回訊息認真一點
variations: 我媽不是不會用網路用語，她只是覺得對家人應該講人話
媽媽的訊息只要超過三秒沒回，她就會打電話問你是不是出事了

en.content:
Mom texted me:
'Hey honey, what does BRB mean?'
Me: 'Be right back.'
Three seconds later:
'Okay. Then please stop saying that to me. Just come back.'
That's the day I learned to text in full sentences.
en.editorNote: For anyone who replies to mom with one-word texts and emojis. Mother's Day reminder: pick up the phone, use real words
variations: Mom doesn't not understand internet slang. She just thinks family deserves actual sentences
If you don't reply to mom within 3 minutes, she assumes you're in a ditch somewhere

items.sourceNote: inspired by the reheated coffee mom-humor trope; rewritten in Taiwanese context

zh-tw.content:
我媽早上泡了一杯咖啡。
中午微波了一次，下午又冰一次，晚上再加熱第三次。
我問她為什麼不重新泡，她說：
「等我有空閒坐下來喝，就是最好的咖啡。」
結果那杯咖啡，從天亮到天黑都沒喝完。
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節最寫實的金句，傳給總是忘記自己也要休息的媽媽。也可以傳給自己當媽的朋友，她們會默默紅了眼眶
variations: 媽媽的咖啡不是飲料，是一個她永遠喝不完的承諾
我媽的杯子裡永遠是冷掉的茶，因為她一直在處理別人的事

en.content:
Mom poured a cup of coffee at 7 a.m.
Reheated it at 10. Forgot it at noon.
Microwaved it again at 3. Found it cold at 6.
I asked why she didn't just make a fresh one.
She said, 'I will. After everyone else is taken care of.'
That coffee never got finished.
en.editorNote: The most accurate Mother's Day quote out there. Send to a mom who forgets she's a person too, or to a friend who just became one — she'll feel seen
variations: Mom's coffee isn't a beverage. It's a promise she never gets around to keeping
Mom's mug is permanently set to 'lukewarm' — that's the temperature of doing everything for everyone else first

items.sourceNote: inspired by the suspicious-silence parenting joke; rewritten with Taiwanese household flavor

zh-tw.content:
小孩在哭，媽媽知道發生什麼事。
小孩在笑，媽媽也大概猜得到。
但小孩突然安靜——
那一刻全家都該緊張起來。
我媽說，育兒最可怕的不是噪音，是沒有聲音。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家裡有小小孩的朋友，她們會邊笑邊點頭。母親節貼文也很適合，懂的人都懂
variations: 小孩太安靜的時候，媽媽的雷達會自動開啟
媽媽的第六感不是迷信，是被三個小孩訓練出來的

en.content:
When the kid cries, mom knows what's wrong.
When the kid laughs, mom usually knows why.
But when the kid goes quiet —
that's when the whole house should be on red alert.
Mom says the scariest sound in parenting isn't noise. It's silence.
en.editorNote: Send this to any parent of toddlers — they'll laugh-cry. Perfect Mother's Day caption for the moms who survived the silent-disaster phase
variations: Three minutes of silence from a toddler equals one Sharpie mural on the wall
Mom's sixth sense isn't magic — it's just years of finding out what 'shhh' actually means

items.sourceNote: inspired by Elizabeth Stone's 'heart walking around outside your body' quote; rewritten as original warm composition

zh-tw.content:
我問我媽：「當媽媽是什麼感覺？」
她想了一下說：
「就是你的心，從此不在你身體裡了。
它跑去上學、跑去工作、跑去你不認識的城市，
然後你每天都要假裝，自己還是好好的。」
我聽完，第一次理解她為什麼晚上會偷哭。
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節最催淚的一則，適合傳給媽媽當卡片內容，或寫在自己離家工作後想她的那個晚上
variations: 媽媽的心，從生下我那天起，就分一半給我帶在身上了
我以為長大就是離家，後來才知道，是把媽媽的心帶著一起走

en.content:
I asked my mom what being a mother feels like.
She thought for a while and said:
'It's having your heart leave your chest. It goes to school, to work, to cities you've never been to.
And every day you pretend you're fine without it.'
That was the first time I understood why she cries quietly at night.
en.editorNote: The Mother's Day card line that wrecks everyone. Use it when words have to do the heavy lifting — birthday, graduation, moving away
variations: Mom's heart split in two the day I was born. I've been carrying half of it around ever since
Growing up wasn't leaving home. It was learning that I took a piece of mom with me every step

items.sourceNote: inspired by Taiwanese mom-fridge humor; original composition

zh-tw.content:
我媽的冰箱有三層哲學：
上層是「等等就吃」，已經放兩個禮拜。
中層是「留給你回來吃」，你回來她又拿出新的。
下層是「我捨不得丟」，連我都不知道那是什麼。
打開冰箱，就像打開她的心。
zh-tw.editorNote: 每個台灣媽媽的冰箱都是一個小宇宙。傳給她，她會笑著否認，但下次回家冰箱還是滿的
variations: 媽媽的冰箱不是冰箱，是時間膠囊
我媽說「快過期了趕快吃」，那盒優格的保存期限其實是上個月

en.content:
My mom's fridge has three layers of philosophy:
Top shelf — 'We'll eat this soon.' It's been there two weeks.
Middle shelf — 'Saved for when you come home.' I come home, she makes new food anyway.
Bottom shelf — 'I just can't throw it out.' Nobody knows what's in there.
Opening her fridge is like opening her heart.
en.editorNote: Every mom's fridge tells the same story. Send to a friend whose mom keeps 'saving food' for them, or post on Mother's Day for instant relatability
variations: Mom's fridge isn't a fridge. It's a time capsule with feelings
Mom says 'eat it before it goes bad.' The expiration date was three weeks ago

items.sourceNote: inspired by Mother's Day 2026 'free room and board' humor; rewritten as original composition

zh-tw.content:
親愛的媽媽：
感謝你提供我這二十幾年來免費的食宿、洗衣、叫起床、心理諮商與外送服務。
如果你願意續約，我這邊隨時都可以簽。
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節傳給媽媽，她看完通常會回一句「續什麼約，你給我自己付房租」，這就是成功
variations: 感謝媽媽長期贊助本人吃、住、洗、哭、笑，續約金免談，繼續耍賴
我媽的客服窗口從未關過，免費還包退換貨

en.content:
Dear Mom,
Thank you for 25+ years of free room, free food, free laundry, free wake-up calls, free therapy, and free DoorDash.
I'd like to renew the contract — terms negotiable but ideally still free.
en.editorNote: Send this on Mother's Day. She'll text back 'pay your own rent' but secretly screenshot it
variations: Mom's been running an all-inclusive resort for me since birth. Five stars, would not check out
Thanks Mom — best customer service in the world, and somehow the refunds are unlimited

items.sourceNote: inspired by mom-text screenshot humor; original composition

zh-tw.content:
我媽的訊息只有三種：
第一種：「在嗎」後面沒有任何事。
第二種：傳一張看不出是什麼的食物照片，問我要不要吃。
第三種：突然傳一個我五歲時的醜照，附註「想到你就笑」。
沒事，就是想我了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給朋友，附一張你媽傳給你的迷之截圖，互相療癒。母親節前後超有共鳴
variations: 媽媽的訊息沒有重點，重點是她想你
我媽傳「在嗎」三十分鐘後才補一句「沒事」，那三十分鐘我心臟跳超快

en.content:
My mom's texts come in three flavors:
1. 'You there?' — followed by absolutely nothing.
2. A blurry photo of food I can't identify, asking if I want some.
3. A random photo of me at age five, looking unhinged, captioned 'thinking of you.'
That's it. That's the message. She just misses me.
en.editorNote: Pair this with a screenshot of your mom's actual text. Universal energy — works any time, peaks around Mother's Day
variations: Mom's texts have no point. The point is that you're the point
My mom sent 'you up?' at 9pm and then nothing for an hour. I aged ten years in that hour

items.sourceNote: inspired by classic Taiwanese mom catchphrases; rewritten as original composition

zh-tw.content:
我媽的三大口頭禪：
「我講過了喔。」（她沒講過）
「隨便你。」（你最好不要真的隨便）
「我又沒有生氣。」（她正在生氣）
媽媽的語言系統，需要二十年才能修完學分。
zh-tw.editorNote: 每個台灣人都能對上號的媽媽翻譯機，貼到家族群組通常會引發大規模認證
variations: 媽媽說「沒關係」的時候，事情通常很有關係
我媽說「你看著辦」的時候，我已經知道答案不是我想的那個

en.content:
My mom has three signature phrases:
'I already told you.' (She did not.)
'Do whatever you want.' (Do not, in fact, do whatever you want.)
'I'm not even mad.' (She is extremely mad.)
Learning to translate mom takes about twenty years and you still fail the final.
en.editorNote: Universal mom-decoder energy. Drop in the family group chat and watch siblings instantly agree
variations: When mom says 'it's fine,' nothing is fine
Mom said 'figure it out yourself.' I figured out the wrong thing. She had opinions

items.sourceNote: inspired by Mother's Day card sentiment about growing into your mom; original composition

zh-tw.content:
小時候我發誓，長大絕對不要變成我媽。
結果三十歲那年，我聽見自己對著貓說：
「你給我下來，這沙發我擦過了。」
語氣、節奏、皺眉的角度，一模一樣。
原來我不是變成她，我是被她養成她。
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節傳給媽媽，她會笑著說「現在知道了喔」，然後晚上多盛你一碗飯
variations: 我以為我逃過了，結果是我把她活成了我自己
我嘴上叫貓的時候，突然懂了我媽當年每一句嘮叨

en.content:
When I was little, I swore I'd never turn into my mom.
Then one day at 30, I heard myself yell at my cat:
'Get off there — I just cleaned that couch.'
The tone. The pause. The exact angle of the eyebrow.
Turns out I didn't become her. She raised me into her, slowly and on purpose.
en.editorNote: Mother's Day card gold. Funny on the surface, sneaks up on you emotionally — perfect for the mom who pretends she's not sentimental
variations: I didn't turn into my mom. She quietly downloaded herself into me over 30 years
Caught myself sighing at the dishwasher exactly the way she does. The transformation is complete

items.sourceNote: inspired by Taiwanese '我數到三' classic mom catchphrase; rewritten as original composition

zh-tw.content:
我媽說「我數到三」的時候，從來沒有真的數到三。
她數到二點五，世界就會發生劇烈變化。
長大後我才知道，那不是倒數，那是預告片。
預告片好看，正片你不會想看。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給有兄弟姊妹的人，大家會立刻回憶起被「二點五」嚇到的童年。家族群組必爆款
variations: 我媽數到三的速度，比我交作業的速度還快
「一、二」之間有十秒鐘，「二、三」之間只有零點三秒

en.content:
When my mom said 'I'm counting to three,' she never actually got to three.
Around two and a half, the room would change temperature.
I didn't realize until adulthood — that wasn't a countdown.
That was a trailer. The movie was much worse.
en.editorNote: Hits anyone who grew up with the 'count to three' threat. Drop it in a sibling group chat for instant nods
variations: She could count from one to three faster than I could turn off the TV
Between 'one' and 'two' was ten seconds. Between 'two' and 'three' was zero

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'mom finds everything' trope; original composition

zh-tw.content:
我找不到東西的時候，我媽會說「就在那裡啊」。
我說「哪裡？」她說「就那裡。」
然後她從我看了三遍的同一個抽屜，把東西拿出來。
我懷疑那個抽屜跟她有合作關係，會把東西藏到我看完才放回去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 每個人都有過「明明就在眼前卻看不見」的崩潰時刻，傳到家族群組大家都會留言+1
variations: 我媽的眼睛內建尋物雷達，我的只能對焦在手機上
找了三天的東西，媽媽兩秒鐘從我房間翻出來，她連房間都不用進

en.content:
When I can't find something, my mom says, 'It's right there.'
I say, 'Where?'
She sighs, walks over, and pulls it out of the exact drawer I checked three times.
I'm convinced the drawer is in on it — it waits until I leave, then puts the thing back.
en.editorNote: Universal — every adult kid recognizes the 'mom-vision' superpower. Great for a relatable Mother's Day post
variations: Mom has built-in tracking. Mine only works on my phone
I searched for three days. She found it in two seconds without entering the room

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'mom is therapist + free wifi' relatable humor; original composition

zh-tw.content:
我媽身兼數職：
白天是我的心理諮商師，晚上是我家的 Wi-Fi 維修員，
中午負責提醒我喝水，半夜負責記得我明天要繳什麼帳單。
月薪零元，年假零天，福利是「兒女有空回家吃飯」。
這種公司早該被勞動部抓去調查了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節傳給媽媽剛剛好，幽默之中帶點不好意思，她會邊看邊笑邊唸「現在才知道喔」
variations: 我媽的職位描述要寫滿一張 A4，薪水欄寫「免費」
我家的後勤總指揮、首席營養師、心理顧問——全部都是同一個人

en.content:
My mom holds multiple roles:
Morning therapist, afternoon Wi-Fi tech support, dinnertime nutritionist, midnight bill-reminder.
Salary: zero. Vacation days: zero. Benefits: 'my kids come home for dinner sometimes.'
This is the kind of company the labor board should really look into.
en.editorNote: A Mother's Day card that lands — funny on the first read, kind of devastating on the second. Send it
variations: Her job description wouldn't fit on a resume. The 'pay' line just says 'lol'
Chief operating officer of my entire life, and somehow she's not on payroll

items.sourceNote: inspired by '我這是為了你好' classic mom phrase; original composition

zh-tw.content:
我媽說「我這是為了你好」的時候，通常後面接的都是我不想做的事。
多穿一件、早點睡、別吃那個、那個誰家的小孩在做什麼。
二十年後我才發現，那些「為我好」累積起來，
就是我現在還活得好好的原因。
媽，對不起，當年我以為你只是囉嗦。
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節貼到 IG 限動或傳給媽媽都很催淚，台灣人尤其有共鳴
variations: 我媽說的「為你好」，當時聽起來像噪音，現在聽起來像保固
原來那些被我嫌煩的話，都是免費版的人生說明書

en.content:
When my mom said 'I'm doing this for your own good,' it was usually followed by something I really didn't want to do.
Wear a jacket. Sleep earlier. Don't eat that. Look at what so-and-so's kid is doing.
It took me twenty years to realize all those 'for your own good's added up —
and they're the reason I'm still standing.
Sorry I thought you were just nagging, Mom.
en.editorNote: Quietly devastating Mother's Day caption — funny intro, emotional landing. Pairs well with an old family photo
variations: Everything she said 'for my own good' turned out to be a warranty I didn't read
The nagging I rolled my eyes at was just the free user manual for adulthood

items.sourceNote: inspired by '37 browser tabs open in mom's brain' trend; original composition

zh-tw.content:
我媽的腦袋裡同時開著三十七個分頁：
冰箱裡剩什麼、誰家小孩結婚、我下週要回家嗎、爸爸的藥吃了沒、
陽台的衣服收了沒、晚上要煮什麼、我從去年欠她的一句謝謝。
而我只能同時想兩件事：肚子餓、想睡覺。
難怪她記得所有事，我連今天禮拜幾都要查。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給朋友吐槽，或母親節傳給媽媽都行，她會笑著說「對啊，你以為我那麼閒喔」
variations: 媽媽的腦袋是雲端硬碟，我的是 16G 容量還快滿了
我媽記得所有人的生日，我連我自己的會員密碼都要重設

en.content:
My mom's brain runs 37 tabs at the same time:
What's in the fridge, whose kid just got married, am I visiting next week, did Dad take his meds,
did she bring the laundry in, what's for dinner, that thank-you I owed her since last year.
Meanwhile my brain runs two tabs: hungry, sleepy.
No wonder she remembers everything and I have to Google what day it is.
en.editorNote: Mother's Day post gold — funny, true, and gently complimentary. Works as caption or card
variations: Mom's brain is cloud storage. Mine is a 16GB drive that's already full
She remembers everyone's birthday. I reset my own password every six weeks

items.sourceNote: inspired by '我數到三' classic mom warning; original composition

zh-tw.content:
我媽說「我數到三」的時候，從來沒有真的數到三。
一、二、二點五、二點七、二點八⋯⋯
那零點二秒，是她留給我反悔的最後機會。
長大才知道，那不是威脅，是她偷偷給我的緩衝時間。
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節傳給媽媽她會笑，分享給朋友大家也都懂。台灣囡仔的集體記憶
variations: 我媽的「三」是有彈性的，她的「最後一次」也是
原來「我數到三」是媽媽版的讀秒，但秒數由愛來決定

en.content:
When my mom said 'I'm counting to three,' she never actually got to three.
One... two... two and a half... two and three quarters...
That extra 0.2 seconds was her quietly giving me one last chance to behave.
Turns out it wasn't a threat — it was a buffer she rigged for me.
en.editorNote: Universal mom move. Works as a nostalgic Mother's Day caption or a tag-your-friends post
variations: My mom's 'three' was flexible. So was her 'last warning'
'I'm counting to three' was just Mom-speak for 'I'll wait, kid'

items.sourceNote: inspired by '別人家的小孩' comparison trope; original composition

zh-tw.content:
我從小就有一個競爭對手，叫做「別人家的小孩」。
他考試永遠考第一，鋼琴會彈，又會幫媽媽洗碗，從來不頂嘴。
後來我長大才發現，別人家的小孩也有一個競爭對手，叫我。
而且他們的媽媽也覺得我比較乖。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給朋友超有共鳴，每個人都當過那個被比較的小孩，也都當過那個「別人家的小孩」
variations: 原來我和「別人家的小孩」一直在對打，輸贏由媽媽們各自宣布
每個媽媽心中都有一個別人家的小孩，剛好那個小孩可能就是你

en.content:
Growing up, I always had one rival: 'the kid next door.'
He got perfect grades, played piano, washed dishes, and never talked back.
Then I grew up and realized the kid next door also had a rival.
It was me. And his mom thought I was the well-behaved one.
en.editorNote: Hits anyone who grew up being compared. Great IG caption with an old childhood photo
variations: Me and 'the kid next door' were locked in a tournament. Our moms kept score separately
Turns out I was someone else's 'the kid next door.' Plot twist of the century

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'mom finds anything' running joke; original composition

zh-tw.content:
我跟我媽說：「媽，我的襪子不見了。」
她坐在客廳沒動，眼睛沒抬，說：「在你抽屜第二格右邊，藍色那雙在最下面。」
我打開，全中。
我懷疑她不是我媽，她是我家的搜尋引擎，而且不用 Wi-Fi。
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節貼這種，媽媽會邊笑邊得意。每個家庭都有這種神奇媽媽
variations: 媽媽的眼睛是 X 光，連藏在床底下的洋芋片都掃得到
我家不需要找東西 App，因為我家有媽媽

en.content:
I told my mom, 'Mom, I can't find my socks.'
Without looking up, she said, 'Second drawer, right side, blue ones are at the bottom.'
I opened it. She was 100% right.
I'm pretty sure she's not my mom — she's the family search engine, and she runs without Wi-Fi.
en.editorNote: Universal mom superpower. Send to your mom on Mother's Day, she'll laugh and feel seen
variations: Mom's eyes have X-ray vision. She finds the chips I hid under the bed
Why download a 'find my stuff' app when my mom exists

items.sourceNote: inspired by '冰箱還有菜' Taiwanese mom pantry culture; original composition

zh-tw.content:
我媽永遠都說：「冰箱還有菜，回家吃。」
但我打開冰箱，看到的是三盒不知道煮多久的滷味、半顆已經有皺紋的高麗菜，
和一個我從來沒看過的玻璃罐。
媽，你說的「還有菜」，跟我理解的「還有菜」可能不是同一個版本。
zh-tw.editorNote: 台灣媽媽的冰箱宇宙，每個遊子打開都會崩潰但同時感動。母親節傳超適合
variations: 我媽的冰箱是個多重宇宙，每個保鮮盒都通往不同的食物時空
媽媽說「還有菜」，是她對家最溫柔的挽留方式

en.content:
My mom always says, 'There's still food in the fridge, just come home and eat.'
Then I open the fridge: three takeout boxes from an unknown era, half a wrinkled cabbage,
and a glass jar I've literally never seen before in my life.
Mom, your definition of 'there's food' and mine might be running on different operating systems.
en.editorNote: Every kid who's moved out gets this. Funny on the surface, gut-punch underneath
variations: Mom's fridge is a multiverse. Each container leads to a different food timeline
'There's food at home' is mom-code for 'please come back'

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'becoming your mother' realization trope; original composition

zh-tw.content:
我以前最怕的一句話是：「你以後就會變得跟你媽一樣。」
二十歲我嗤之以鼻，三十歲我開始進冷氣房叫別人「把外套穿上」，
進超市先看特價，看到別人的小孩會忍不住說「這麼瘦要多吃一點」。
媽，我投降。原來變成你不是詛咒，是升級。
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節必傳，媽媽看到會邊笑邊說「我就跟你說吧」。三十歲以上特別有感
variations: 我曾經發誓不要變成我媽，現在我連碎念的順序都跟她一樣
長大後最大的劇情反轉，是發現我媽其實是對的

en.content:
The line I feared most growing up was: 'One day you're going to turn into your mother.'
At twenty, I laughed. At thirty, I'm telling strangers to put a jacket on,
checking grocery flyers for sales, and muttering 'that kid needs to eat more' at the playground.
Mom, I surrender. Turning into you wasn't a curse — it was an upgrade.
en.editorNote: Perfect for a Mother's Day post — funny opening, soft landing. Hits hardest for anyone over 30
variations: Swore I'd never become my mom. Now I nag in the exact same word order
Biggest plot twist of adulthood: realizing she was right the whole time

items.sourceNote: inspired by the universal 'we'll see' mom-ism; original composition

zh-tw.content:
我媽說「再說」的時候，其實是「不行」。
我媽說「隨便你」的時候，其實是「你最好給我想清楚」。
我媽說「沒事」的時候，是全宇宙最有事的時刻。
中文是她的母語，但媽媽語才是她的第一語言。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給朋友會被秒回「我媽也是這樣！」屬於跨家庭通用的共鳴系列
variations: 媽媽的「我跟你說最後一次」，通常是當天的第七次
翻譯媽媽語要學十年，畢業後還會發現有進階版

en.content:
When mom says 'we'll see,' she means 'no.'
When mom says 'do whatever you want,' she means 'you'd better think this through.'
When mom says 'I'm fine,' the entire household goes into red alert.
English is her language, but Mom-ese is her mother tongue.
en.editorNote: Post this anywhere and watch the 'OMG MY MOM TOO' comments roll in. Universal across every family
variations: Mom's 'I'm telling you for the last time' is usually the seventh time that day
Took me 20 years to learn Mom-ese. Turns out there's an advanced course

items.sourceNote: inspired by the 'it's spicy' shared-snack trope; original composition

zh-tw.content:
小時候我媽說「這個很辣，你不能吃」的東西，長大後我發現：
通常一點都不辣，只是她不想分。
媽，沒關係，我懂了，也原諒你了。
但那盒草莓蛋糕到底辣在哪裡，可以給我一個交代嗎？
zh-tw.editorNote: 童年陰影系列，看到的人都會大喊「我也被騙過！」適合家庭群組或母親節閒聊
variations: 我媽說「這個是大人吃的」，翻譯：「這個我自己要吃」
「這個小孩吃了會睡不著」是另一個經典謊言，事後證明只有她吃了會開心

en.content:
Growing up, anything mom said was 'too spicy for you' turned out to be:
not spicy at all — she just didn't want to share.
Mom, it's okay. I forgive you.
But I'm still going to need an explanation for that strawberry shortcake.
en.editorNote: Pure childhood betrayal — everyone has a version of this. Great for the family group chat
variations: 'This is grown-up food' was mom's polite way of saying 'this is MY food'
'It'll keep you up at night' is the lie. The truth: it kept HER happy

items.sourceNote: inspired by mom-as-tech-support humor; original composition

zh-tw.content:
我媽用手機的方式很特別：
打電話一定要開擴音，全社區都聽得到。
傳訊息一定要打標點符號，每句後面加三個句號。。。
看到不認識的通知就傳給我問「這是不是詐騙」，連天氣預報都問過一次。
但每次我回家，她的Wi-Fi密碼背得比我還熟。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳到家庭群組會被媽媽已讀不回，但她其實有偷笑。三十歲以上的小孩特別有共鳴
variations: 我媽的手機桌面有四十個資料夾，每個都叫「新資料夾」
教我媽用LINE那天，我多長了三根白頭髮

en.content:
My mom uses her phone in her own special way:
Every call is on speaker. The whole neighborhood is in on it.
Every text ends with three periods. No exceptions...
Every unknown notification is sent to me with 'is this a scam?' — including, once, the weather alert.
But when I come home, she knows the Wi-Fi password better than I do.
en.editorNote: Send this to the family chat. Mom will leave it on read but secretly laugh. Hits hard for the over-30 crowd
variations: My mom has 40 folders on her home screen. All named 'New Folder'
Teaching mom to use WhatsApp added three gray hairs to my head

items.sourceNote: inspired by the 'mom never sits down' observation; original composition

zh-tw.content:
我這輩子沒看過我媽坐下來好好吃一餐飯。
她總是站著，盛飯、夾菜、補湯、擦桌，
等到所有人吃飽了才坐下來吃剩菜。
媽，今年母親節換我們站著，妳坐下。
妳的功課做完了，可以下課了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節重磅級，前面笑著看完，最後一句會鼻酸。傳給媽媽幾乎一定會被收藏
variations: 媽媽的位置永遠是離廚房最近的那個
今年母親節，第一個動筷子的人請是媽媽

en.content:
I've never once seen my mom sit through a whole meal.
She's up — refilling rice, passing dishes, topping off soup, wiping the table —
and finally sits to eat what's left after everyone else is full.
Mom, this Mother's Day, you sit. We'll stand.
Class dismissed. You did the homework.
en.editorNote: A Mother's Day heavy hitter — laughs first, lump-in-throat at the end. Almost guaranteed to get saved
variations: Mom's seat at dinner is always the one closest to the kitchen
This year, mom takes the first bite. That's the rule

items.sourceNote: inspired by the 'mom = therapist + Wi-Fi' joke; original composition

zh-tw.content:
我媽身兼數職：
廚師、清潔工、心理諮商師、家庭IT、免費保母、財務顧問。
薪水：零元。
年資：我活幾年她做幾年。
離職率：零。
媽，這份工作妳真的做太久了，今天放妳半天假。
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節傳給媽媽看，最後一句是關鍵。也可以傳到家庭群組，讓爸爸看了會反省
variations: 我媽的工作說明書如果寫出來，HR會說這份工作違法
媽媽是這個家唯一沒有勞基法保障的員工

en.content:
My mom wears a lot of hats:
Chef, housekeeper, therapist, family IT, free babysitter, financial advisor.
Salary: zero.
Years of service: however old I am.
Turnover rate: zero.
Mom, you've held this job way too long. Take the afternoon off.
en.editorNote: Send to mom for Mother's Day — the last line lands. Also great for the family chat, gets dads thinking
variations: If mom's job description were written out, HR would say it's illegal
Mom is the only person in this house with no labor protections

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'mom's selective hearing' humor; original composition

zh-tw.content:
我媽的聽力很神奇：
我在房間小聲講電話，她聽得到。
我在客廳大聲喊「媽我餓了」，她聽不到。
半夜兩點冰箱門開三秒，她聽得到。
叫她去看醫生講十遍，她聽不到。
媽，妳的選擇性失聰，掛號費我幫妳付。
zh-tw.editorNote: 全家有共鳴的萬用梗，傳給兄弟姊妹一定會被秒回。最後一句的關心藏得剛剛好
variations: 我媽的耳朵是宇宙最精密的設備，只是頻率設定很奇怪
媽媽聽得到我沒講出口的嘆氣，聽不到我請她休息的請求

en.content:
My mom's hearing is a wonder of science:
I whisper on the phone in my room — she hears it.
I yell 'Mom, I'm hungry' from the couch — nothing.
Fridge door opens for three seconds at 2 a.m. — she hears it.
Told her ten times to see a doctor — silence.
Mom, your selective hearing has a co-pay. I'll cover it.
en.editorNote: Universal sibling chat material — send it and watch the replies roll in. The last line sneaks in real concern
variations: My mom's ears are the most advanced equipment in the universe, just tuned to weird frequencies
Mom hears the sigh I didn't say out loud, but not when I ask her to rest

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'mom's fridge as time capsule' observation; original composition

zh-tw.content:
我媽的冰箱是一個平行宇宙：
最上層有一罐我國中買的果醬。
冷凍庫有一塊不知道是肉還是石頭的東西。
保鮮盒裡有三個顏色，分別是「上週的菜」「上上週的菜」「我不敢問的菜」。
但只要我回家，她永遠變得出一桌熱的、新鮮的、剛好我愛吃的。
媽，妳的冰箱有魔法。
zh-tw.editorNote: 前面好笑，後面溫暖，傳給媽媽她會回「亂講」但其實在偷笑。回家前傳特別應景
variations: 我媽冰箱裡的東西比我活的時間還久
回家最幸福的事：打開鍋蓋，裡面有我最愛吃的那一道

en.content:
My mom's fridge is a parallel universe:
Top shelf: jam I bought in middle school.
Freezer: something that may be meat, may be a rock.
Three containers in the back: 'last week's leftovers,' 'two weeks ago,' and 'I'm afraid to ask.'
But the second I come home, she pulls out a hot, fresh meal of exactly what I love.
Mom, your fridge has magic.
en.editorNote: Funny first, warm second. Send to mom — she'll say 'stop it' but she's smiling. Perfect right before a trip home
variations: Stuff in my mom's fridge has outlived parts of my life
Best part of coming home: lifting the lid and seeing your favorite dish underneath

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'mom worries forever' theme; original composition

zh-tw.content:
小時候我以為媽媽是無所不能的超人。
長大後才發現，她其實也會累、會怕、會偷哭。
只是她每次擦完眼淚，還是會問我「你今天吃飽了沒」。
媽，妳不用一直當超人。
今年母親節，換我當妳的後盾。
zh-tw.editorNote: 走心型，適合長大後比較懂事的小孩。母親節當天傳剛剛好，太早傳眼淚會先到
variations: 媽媽不是超人，她只是因為愛我才一直撐著
今年母親節，我想跟媽媽說：妳可以軟一點，我撐得住

en.content:
As a kid, I thought my mom was a superhero who couldn't be tired.
Growing up, I learned she gets tired, gets scared, cries when no one's looking.
She just always wipes her eyes and asks, 'Did you eat today?'
Mom, you don't have to be the superhero anymore.
This Mother's Day, let me be the one who's got you.
en.editorNote: A tender one — best for grown-up kids who finally get it. Send it on the actual day; any earlier and the tears come too soon
variations: Mom isn't a superhero — she just kept going because she loves me
This Mother's Day, mom, you can be soft. I've got you

items.sourceNote: inspired by viral 'ominous parent text' trend; rewritten as an original take on mom's vague-but-deadly opening messages

zh-tw.content:
我媽傳訊息的開頭只有三種：
「在嗎？」——可能是要傳水果照片。
「打給我。」——可能是想問我吃了沒。
「沒事，不重要。」——一定有事，而且很重要。

看到第三種我會直接打電話過去，因為我已經被訓練了三十年。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給朋友群組大家會秒懂，或傳給媽媽讓她笑著說「我才沒有」。母親節前後的閒聊神器
variations: 我媽傳「沒事」的時候，是我心跳最快的時候
我媽的訊息只有兩個風險等級：低風險和未知

en.content:
My mom's texts start one of three ways:
'You there?' — probably a fruit photo incoming.
'Call me.' — probably wants to ask if I ate.
'Nothing important, never mind.' — definitely important. Always.

I call back on sight for the third one. Thirty years of training.
en.editorNote: Drop in the group chat and watch friends nod. Or send to your mom for a 'I do not do that' response. Easy Mother's Day icebreaker
variations: When my mom texts 'never mind,' my heart rate spikes
My mom's texts have two threat levels: low and unknown

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'mom's 37 mental tabs' theme; rewritten with an original task-manager metaphor

zh-tw.content:
我媽不是用記事本管理家裡的事，
她是直接把所有東西放在腦袋裡跑。

誰幾號要打疫苗、冰箱什麼快過期、誰最近沒打電話回來、那個誰生日是這個月還是下個月⋯⋯

她沒有當機過，
只是偶爾會嘆一口氣然後說「我頭好痛」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給媽媽她會說「對啊我頭真的很痛」，傳給朋友大家會說「我媽也是」。日常分享題材
variations: 我媽的腦袋是家裡唯一不會斷線的系統
我媽沒記過行事曆，但她從來沒漏掉任何一個生日

en.content:
My mom doesn't use a planner.
She runs the entire household directly in her head.

Whose vaccine is due, what's about to expire in the fridge, who hasn't called back in two weeks, whether someone's birthday is this month or next…

She's never crashed.
She just occasionally sighs and says, 'My head hurts.'
en.editorNote: Send to your mom and she'll say 'yeah, it really does.' Send to friends and they'll say 'mine too.' Universal mom content
variations: My mom's brain is the only system in our house that doesn't lag
She's never written down a birthday and she's never missed one

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'mom is the lost-and-found' humor trope; rewritten as an original scene about mom remembering everything you forget

zh-tw.content:
我問我媽：「我國中那件外套還在嗎？」
她說：「在啊，左邊櫃子第二層，後面藍色那個袋子裡。」

我自己昨天放的鑰匙找不到。
她說：「在你右手邊的桌上。」

我懷疑我媽的記憶力不是天生的，
是我每丟一次東西，她就升一級。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在家庭群組分享或傳給有同感的朋友，媽媽看到會得意。也適合配一個媽媽找東西的合照
variations: 我媽的記憶力像是我家的雲端備份，永遠不會壞
找不到的東西不是不見了，只是還沒去問我媽

en.content:
I asked my mom, 'Is my middle school jacket still around?'
She said, 'Yeah — second shelf on the left, in the blue bag at the back.'

Meanwhile, I can't find the keys I put down yesterday.
She says, 'On the table. To your right.'

I'm starting to think her memory isn't natural.
I think every time I lose something, she levels up.
en.editorNote: Post in the family chat or send to friends — mom will smile smugly. Pair with a photo of her digging through a closet
variations: My mom's memory is the cloud backup our house was built on
Things aren't lost. They're just not found until you ask mom

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'mom's worry never ends, even as you grow up' theme; rewritten as an original observation about mom calling 'be careful' decades after you've left home

zh-tw.content:
我已經三十幾歲了，
出門前我媽還是會說：「路上小心。」
下雨時還是會說：「帶傘。」
晚回家還是會說：「下次早一點。」

我有時候會笑她：「媽，我又不是小孩了。」
她說：「對我來說你就是啊。」

那一句我沒回，因為我知道她說的是真的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節走心款，傳給媽媽她可能會回一個害羞的表情符號。也適合貼在朋友圈讓大家一起鼻酸
variations: 對媽媽來說，孩子永遠是那個剛學會走路的版本
我長大了，但在我媽眼裡，我永遠需要她提醒帶傘

en.content:
I'm in my thirties now.
Before I leave the house, my mom still says, 'Be careful out there.'
When it rains: 'Take an umbrella.'
When I'm home late: 'Try to be earlier next time.'

Sometimes I laugh and say, 'Mom, I'm not a kid anymore.'
She says, 'You are to me.'

I never have a comeback. Because she's right.
en.editorNote: A tender one for Mother's Day — send and she might reply with a shy emoji. Also lands well as a quiet share
variations: To mom, you'll always be the version of you that just learned to walk
I grew up. In my mom's eyes, I still need a reminder to bring an umbrella

items.sourceNote: inspired by the 'mom says she's fine' trope; rewritten as an original observation about mom's automatic 'I'm okay' response

zh-tw.content:
我媽說「我沒事」的時候，通常代表三件事：
第一，她有事。
第二，她不想讓我擔心。
第三，她已經自己處理一半了。

後來我學會了一招：
聽到「我沒事」，我會回「那我等一下打電話給妳」。
她會嘆一口氣，然後說真話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 走心型，母親節傳給媽媽剛剛好。也可以傳給朋友提醒大家：媽媽的「沒事」是要翻譯的
variations: 媽媽的「我沒事」是這世界上最不該被相信的三個字
我媽說沒事，我聽出來有事，這是我們之間的暗號

en.content:
When my mom says 'I'm fine,' it usually means three things:
One, she's not.
Two, she doesn't want me to worry.
Three, she's already halfway through fixing it herself.

I learned the trick eventually.
When she says 'I'm fine,' I say 'I'll call you in a minute.'
She sighs. Then she tells me the truth.
en.editorNote: Tender one — good for Mother's Day, or as a reminder to friends that 'I'm fine' from mom always needs translating
variations: 'I'm fine' is the most untrustworthy sentence in my mom's vocabulary
When mom says she's fine, I know she isn't. That's our code

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'mom's camera roll is 99% kids' meme trope; rewritten as an original observation about mom's phone as a family archive

zh-tw.content:
我滑開我媽的手機相簿：
我小學畢業照、我國中比讚的照片、我大學亂入別人鏡頭的照片、我穿西裝那次、我抱寵物的那次、還有大概三千張我吃飯的照片。

她自己的自拍：兩張。
一張是2018年她去剪頭髮拍的，另一張是不小心按到的。

媽，下次我們一起拍。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給媽媽她會說「你看我相簿做什麼」，但其實她很開心。母親節幫媽媽自拍一張剛剛好
variations: 我媽的相簿裡只有我和食物，沒有她自己
母親節最好的禮物之一：一張你跟媽媽的合照

en.content:
I scrolled through my mom's camera roll:
My elementary school graduation photo. Me throwing a peace sign in middle school. Me photobombing strangers in college. Me in a suit that one time. Me holding the dog. And roughly three thousand pictures of me eating.

Her own selfies: two.
One from a 2018 haircut. One she took by accident.

Mom, next time we take one together.
en.editorNote: Send to your mom — she'll say 'why are you looking at my photos' but she's smiling. Pair it with a selfie of the two of you on Mother's Day
variations: My mom's photo album is me and food. She's not in any of them
Best Mother's Day gift: a photo where she's actually in the frame too

items.sourceNote: inspired by the 'mom translates weather into instructions' parenting trope; rewritten as an original take on mom's weather-app interpretation skills

zh-tw.content:
我媽看氣象預報的方式跟一般人不一樣：

氣溫22度 →「會冷，加外套。」
氣溫28度 →「會熱，多喝水。」
降雨機率30% →「會下，帶傘。」
降雨機率0% →「也可能下，還是帶傘。」

我媽不是看天氣，她是把天氣翻譯成對我的指令。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳到家庭群組，兄弟姊妹會秒回「我媽也是」。也很適合配一張媽媽看手機的照片
variations: 我媽的氣象APP有特殊版本，每個數字後面都會自動跳出一句叮嚀
不管降雨機率多少，我媽的結論永遠是「帶傘」

en.content:
My mom reads weather forecasts in a completely different language than the rest of us:

72°F → 'It's chilly, bring a jacket.'
82°F → 'It's hot, drink water.'
30% chance of rain → 'It might rain, take an umbrella.'
0% chance of rain → 'It could still rain, take one anyway.'

She's not reading weather. She's translating it into instructions for me.
en.editorNote: Drop in the family chat — your siblings will respond instantly with 'same.' Pair with a photo of mom on her phone
variations: My mom's weather app has a secret edition where every number comes with a reminder
No matter the forecast, mom's conclusion is always the same: take an umbrella

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'mom says she already ate' trope; rewritten as an original quiet observation about mom's quiet sacrifice during meals

zh-tw.content:
小時候桌上只剩最後一塊雞腿，我媽永遠說：「我吃過了，你吃。」

後來我才發現，她那天根本還沒坐下來吃飯。
她吃過的是廚房的鍋邊菜、是我們剩下的、是站著吃完的那一口。

媽，今年我要先夾起那塊雞腿，放到妳碗裡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節走心款，傳完媽媽通常會回「亂講我真的有吃」。其實她沒有，你也知道
variations: 媽媽的「我吃過了」是這世界上最常見的善意謊言
今年的母親節，我要看著媽媽吃完最後一口飯

en.content:
When there was one piece of chicken left on the table, my mom always said: 'I already ate, you have it.'

Years later I realized she hadn't sat down at all that day.
What she 'ate' was the burned edge from the pan, the leftovers nobody wanted, the bite she stood up to swallow in the kitchen.

Mom, this year I'm putting the chicken in your bowl first.
en.editorNote: Mother's Day heart-hitter. She'll say 'no really, I did eat.' She didn't. You know it. Send anyway
variations: 'I already ate' is the most common kind lie in the world
This Mother's Day, I'm watching her finish her own plate before I touch mine

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'mom buys for everyone but herself' theme; rewritten as an original portrait of mom's selective frugality

zh-tw.content:
我媽逛街的標準：
我的鞋子破了一個小洞 →「不能穿了，明天去買新的。」
她自己的拖鞋少了一塊鞋底 →「還可以穿幾年。」
我說想吃某家餐廳 →「走吧，今天我請。」
她想喝杯咖啡 →「外面太貴，回家泡就好。」

媽，今年我們去那家妳上次說太貴的咖啡廳。
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節傳這句然後真的帶她去，比任何禮物都實在。媽媽會嘴上說「浪費錢」，但心裡很開心
variations: 我媽對自己最節省，對我最捨得，這個算式一直都不平衡
今年母親節我學會一件事：媽媽說「不用啦」的東西，最值得買

en.content:
My mom's shopping logic:
My shoe has a small hole → 'You can't wear that, we're buying new ones tomorrow.'
Her slipper is missing a sole → 'A few more years left in this.'
I mention a restaurant I like → 'Let's go, my treat.'
She wants a coffee → 'Too expensive out, I'll make some at home.'

Mom, this year we're going to that café you said was too expensive.
en.editorNote: Send this and actually take her — better than any gift. She'll say 'such a waste,' but inside she's beaming
variations: Mom is cheapest with herself, most generous with me. The math has never balanced
Mother's Day lesson: whatever mom says 'don't bother' about — that's the thing worth buying

items.sourceNote: inspired by the universal 'do you think we own the electric company / turn off the lights' parental trope; rewritten as an original portrait of a mom patrolling the house

zh-tw.content:
我媽走過家裡每個房間，順手就把燈關掉。
「沒人在你開燈做什麼？」「電費是用印的喔？」

我以為那只是唸。
直到我自己租房子收到第一張電費單，
我站在客廳，默默把走廊的燈關了。

原來那不是嘮叨，那是預言。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給已經自己付電費的朋友，大家都會心一笑。母親節也可以傳給媽媽承認「妳是對的」
variations: 搬出去之後我才發現，我媽關燈的手速是練出來的生存技能
我媽這輩子最大的敵人不是別人，是家裡每一盞沒關的燈

en.content:
My mom walks through every room flipping off lights without breaking stride.
'Nobody's in here, why is this on?' 'You think we print money for the power bill?'

I thought it was just nagging.
Then I got my first electric bill in my own apartment.
I stood in the living room and quietly turned off the hallway light.

Turns out it wasn't nagging. It was a prophecy.
en.editorNote: Send to friends who now pay their own utility bills — instant recognition. Or send to your mom to admit she was right all along
variations: After moving out I realized my mom's light-switch reflexes were a survival skill
My mom's lifelong nemesis isn't a person. It's every light left on in the house

items.sourceNote: inspired by the classic '去問你爸' mom catchphrase from Taiwanese mom-quote roundups; rewritten as an original observation about the family decision loop

zh-tw.content:
小時候我問我媽可不可以，
她說：「去問你爸。」
我去問我爸，
他說：「問你媽啊。」

我在他們兩個之間跑了好幾年，
長大才懂——
那不是踢皮球，
那是他們其中一個不想當壞人，又捨不得拒絕我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給有兄弟姊妹的人，這個迴圈大家都跑過。母親節傳給媽媽她可能會笑著說「哪有」
variations: 「去問你爸」的真正意思是：我其實想答應，但不想負責
我家的決策流程是一個無限迴圈，而我就是那個被丟來丟去的封包

en.content:
When I was a kid and asked my mom for permission,
she'd say, 'Go ask your dad.'
So I'd ask my dad,
and he'd say, 'Ask your mom.'

I bounced between them for years.
It took me until adulthood to understand —
it wasn't them dodging me.
One of them just didn't want to be the bad guy, but couldn't bear to say no either.
en.editorNote: Send to anyone with siblings — everyone ran this loop. Your mom will laugh and say 'we did not do that'
variations: 'Go ask your dad' actually means: I want to say yes, but I don't want to be responsible for it
My family's decision process was an infinite loop, and I was the packet being passed back and forth

items.sourceNote: inspired by the reverse-psychology mom line '不要再看書了，眼睛會壞掉，趕快過來看電視' from Taiwanese funny mom-quote roundups; rewritten as an original tender twist about a mom worrying you work too hard

zh-tw.content:
別人的媽媽叫小孩去讀書。
我媽看我在唸書，走過來說：「別讀了啦，眼睛會壞，過來吃個水果休息一下。」

小時候我以為我媽很奇怪。
長大才發現，
她不是不在乎我的成績，
她只是更在乎我這個人有沒有好好活著。
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節傳給媽媽剛剛好：前半段好笑，後半段走心。她看了大概會說「廢話，你比成績重要」
variations: 我媽催我休息的次數，比催我用功的次數多很多
別人家的媽媽怕小孩不夠拚，我媽怕我太拚

en.content:
Other people's moms tell their kids to go study.
My mom sees me studying, walks over, and says: 'Stop reading, you'll ruin your eyes — come have some fruit and take a break.'

As a kid I thought my mom was weird.
As an adult I finally got it.
She wasn't indifferent to my grades.
She just cared more about whether I, the actual person, was okay.
en.editorNote: A great Mother's Day send — funny first half, tender landing. She'll probably reply 'obviously, you matter more than a grade'
variations: My mom told me to rest way more often than she told me to study
Other moms worry their kid isn't trying hard enough. Mine worried I tried too hard

items.sourceNote: inspired by the 'leftovers — from when? / emotionally from 2019' mom-joke trope; rewritten as an original bit about a mom's fridge management philosophy

zh-tw.content:
我媽的冰箱有自己的時間軸。
我問：「這個還能吃嗎？」
她說：「可以啦，前天剛煮的。」
我問：「那這個呢？」
她想了三秒：「應該……也是前天。」

在我媽的世界裡，所有東西都是「前天剛煮的」，
包含那盒我確定上禮拜就在的滷肉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在家庭群組分享絕對引起共鳴，每個人都有一個冰箱時間觀很模糊的媽媽。媽媽看到會反駁「我都有在清」
variations: 我媽的冰箱不是冰箱，是一個沒有人敢深入考古的遺跡
「丟掉很浪費」是我媽和她冰箱之間最堅定的盟約

en.content:
My mom's fridge runs on its own timeline.
I asked, 'Is this still good to eat?'
She said, 'Yeah, I just made that the other day.'
I asked, 'What about this one?'
She thought for three seconds: 'That's… also the other day.'

In my mom's universe, everything was 'just made the other day' —
including the braised pork I'm pretty sure has been there since last week.
en.editorNote: Drop this in the family group chat for instant agreement — everyone has a mom whose fridge timeline is a mystery. She'll insist 'I clean it out all the time'
variations: My mom's fridge isn't a fridge. It's an archaeological site no one is brave enough to excavate
'It's a waste to throw it out' is the unshakable treaty between my mom and her fridge

items.sourceNote: inspired by the viral trend of parents sending hilariously ominous one-line texts to their adult kids; rewritten as an original bit

zh-tw.content:
我媽傳訊息的標點符號比她的內容還可怕。
「回個電話。」
句號。
沒有表情符號、沒有解釋、沒有下一句。

我立刻把這輩子做過的虧心事複習了一遍，
打電話回去，她說：「沒事啦，就問你晚餐想吃什麼。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 在家庭群組或朋友群分享必中，每個成年小孩都被媽媽的「句號簡訊」嚇過。媽媽看了會說「我哪有」
variations: 我媽一句「在嗎」，可以讓我心跳漏三拍，結果只是要問我家用網路怎麼設定
媽媽的訊息越簡短，我越覺得自己要被請去喝茶

en.content:
My mom's punctuation is scarier than anything she actually says.
'Call me.'
Period.
No emoji, no explanation, no follow-up text.

I immediately reviewed every bad thing I've ever done,
called her back, and she said: 'Oh, nothing — just wondering what you want for dinner.'
en.editorNote: Guaranteed hit in the family or friend group chat — every adult kid has been spooked by mom's one-line 'period text.' She'll reply 'I do not do that'
variations: A single 'you up?' from my mom can drop my heart three beats, and then she just needs help with the wifi
The shorter my mom's text, the more convinced I am I'm about to be summoned

items.sourceNote: inspired by the universal panic of receiving 'we need to talk' with no punctuation; rewritten as an original bit

zh-tw.content:
三十歲了，我媽傳「我們需要談談」，
我還是會瞬間從一個成年人變回小學生。

腦中跑馬燈：是哪句話講錯了？是誰跟她告狀？是不是上次回家忘了關燈？

結果她要談的是——
「電鍋的內鍋你上次放哪了，我找不到。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給同樣有「被媽媽訊息嚇大」經驗的朋友，共鳴度爆表。母親節傳給媽媽也行，她會笑說「你想太多」
variations: 「我們需要談談」這六個字，我媽用過最嚴重的一次，是要問我退休基金怎麼買
媽媽的「談談」範圍可大可小，從人生規劃到醬油快沒了都有可能

en.content:
I'm thirty, and when my mom texts 'we need to talk' with no punctuation,
I still instantly revert from a functioning adult back to a third-grader.

My brain starts the slideshow: what did I say wrong? Who told on me? Did I forget to turn off a light last time I visited?

Turns out what she needed to talk about was:
'Where did you put the inner pot of the rice cooker? I can't find it.'
en.editorNote: Send to friends who also grew up jumpy from mom's texts — instant relatability. Works as a Mother's Day send too; she'll laugh and say 'you overthink everything'
variations: The most serious thing my mom ever opened with 'we need to talk' was a question about my retirement fund
My mom's 'talk' could be anything from life decisions to 'we're almost out of soy sauce'

items.sourceNote: inspired by the 'call mom to ask how long the chicken stays in the oven after a decade on your own' trope; rewritten as an original bit

zh-tw.content:
我已經自己一個人住超過十年了，
有自己的工作、自己的房租、自己的人生決定。

但每次煮飯卡關，我第一個打的還是我媽：
「雞肉要烤幾分鐘？」「滷蛋要泡多久？」「這個是不是壞掉了？」

媽媽就是那個不管你幾歲，永遠是你人生客服專線的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節傳給媽媽剛剛好，前面好笑、後面走心。她大概會回「打來就好，問這些幹嘛客氣」
variations: 我手機裡按通話鍵最快的人，是我媽，而且通常是因為廚房出事了
長大不是不再需要媽媽，是改成需要她遠端指導我怎麼把飯煮熟

en.content:
I've lived on my own for over ten years now.
I have my own job, my own rent, my own life decisions.

But the second I get stuck cooking, the first person I call is still my mom:
'How long does the chicken stay in the oven?' 'How long do the eggs soak?' 'Is this one gone bad?'

A mom is the person who stays your lifelong customer-support line, no matter how old you get.
en.editorNote: A great Mother's Day send — funny up front, tender at the end. She'll probably reply 'just call, don't be shy about it'
variations: The person I can dial fastest on my phone is my mom — usually because something is going wrong in the kitchen
Growing up didn't mean I stopped needing my mom. It just means I now need her for remote cooking support

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'motherhood is 90% looking for things your kids just had in their hands'; rewritten as an original bit

zh-tw.content:
當媽以後我才發現，
人生有百分之九十的時間，都在找小孩三秒前還拿在手上的東西。

「我的襪子呢？」在你腳上。
「我的水壺呢？」在你旁邊。
「我的玩具呢？」在你另一隻手上。

我不是媽媽，我是一個全年無休的失物招領處。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在媽媽社群或家庭群組分享超有共鳴，每個帶過小孩的人都懂這種日常崩潰。當媽的朋友看到會狂點頭
variations: 當媽的超能力不是無所不能，是能找到小孩自己弄丟、還堅稱是我藏起來的東西
「東西不見了」在我家通常代表「東西就在你手上，你只是沒在看」

en.content:
Since becoming a mom, I've realized
ninety percent of my life is spent finding things my kid was holding three seconds ago.

'Where are my socks?' On your feet.
'Where's my water bottle?' Right next to you.
'Where's my toy?' In your other hand.

I'm not a mom. I'm a 24/7 lost-and-found department.
en.editorNote: Drop this in a mom group or family chat for instant nods — every parent of small kids knows this exact meltdown. Mom friends will agree hard
variations: A mom's superpower isn't doing everything. It's finding the thing the kid lost and then swears I hid
'It's gone' in my house usually means 'it's in your hand, you're just not looking'

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'mom life: reheated coffee and unfinished sentences / powered by love, caffeine and mild confusion'; rewritten as an original bit

zh-tw.content:
我媽的咖啡從來沒有在熱的時候喝完過。
泡好、放著、忙小孩、回來、再微波、再放著、再忙、再微波。

一杯咖啡，可以陪她過完一整個早上。

當媽的日常就是：靠愛、咖啡因，跟一點點搞不清楚狀況，撐完每一天。
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節傳給媽媽會心一笑，或在媽媽群組分享都很合適。她看到大概會說「對欸我那杯咖啡現在還在微波爐裡」
variations: 我媽喝的不是咖啡，是一杯被微波過四次、象徵她整個早上的紀念品
當媽以後，一句話講到一半被打斷，已經變成我媽的標準說話方式

en.content:
My mom has never once finished her coffee while it was still hot.
Brew it, set it down, deal with a kid, come back, microwave it, set it down, get busy, microwave it again.

One cup of coffee can keep her company for an entire morning.

Mom life: running on love, caffeine, and a mild, permanent state of confusion.
en.editorNote: A warm little laugh for a Mother's Day send, or perfect for a mom group. She'll probably say 'oh no, my coffee is still in the microwave right now'
variations: My mom doesn't drink coffee. She drinks a four-times-reheated souvenir of her entire morning
Since becoming a mom, getting cut off mid-sentence has just become her normal way of talking

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'nothing is truly lost until Mom can't find it'; rewritten as original bit

zh-tw.content:
東西在我手邊找十分鐘找不到，崩潰大喊「不見了」。
我媽走過來，看都沒看，手往旁邊一伸就拿給我。

在這個家裡，東西不是真的不見，是要等我媽來認證它存在。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳到家庭群組大家都會點頭，母親節傳給媽媽她會驕傲地說「不然咧」。配一句『媽你眼睛裝雷達喔』更好笑
variations: 我家的搜尋引擎不是 Google，是我媽，而且零秒回應、命中率百分之百
東西在我面前我看不到，在我媽腦袋裡她記得清清楚楚，這是什麼超能力

en.content:
I search for ten minutes, find nothing, and yell that it's gone forever.
Mom walks over, doesn't even look, reaches one hand to the side, and hands it to me.

In this house, nothing is actually lost. It just hasn't been confirmed to exist by Mom yet.
en.editorNote: Family group chat gold — everyone nods. Send it to your mom on Mother's Day and she'll just say 'obviously.' Pairs well with 'how do your eyes have radar'
variations: Our home search engine isn't Google, it's Mom — zero latency, one hundred percent hit rate
It's invisible to me right in front of my face, but Mom has it memorized. What kind of superpower is that

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'the quickest way to get a child's attention is to sit down'; rewritten and flipped to the mom's POV

zh-tw.content:
我媽忙了一整天，終於坐下來，屁股還沒碰到沙發。
那一秒，全家人同時想起有事要找她：誰的襪子、晚餐吃什麼、遙控器在哪。

科學證實：媽媽坐下，是全宇宙最強的召喚術。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在家庭群組逗大家笑，也適合提醒自己『媽媽坐下時就讓她坐著』。母親節貼文配一張媽媽終於癱在沙發上的照片超有梗
variations: 讓媽媽永遠休息不了的方法很簡單：等她一坐下就好
我媽這輩子沒坐滿過一張沙發，因為她每次坐下都會被『召喚』起來

en.content:
Mom's been busy all day. She finally sits down. Her butt hasn't even touched the couch yet.
That exact second, the whole family suddenly remembers they need her: whose socks, what's for dinner, where's the remote.

Science confirms it: a mom sitting down is the most powerful summoning spell in the universe.
en.editorNote: A guaranteed laugh in the family chat, and a gentle reminder to let Mom actually stay sitting. Great for a Mother's Day post with a photo of her finally collapsed on the couch
variations: Want to make sure Mom never rests? Easy — just wait for the moment she sits down
My mom has never once fully sat on a couch, because every time she sits she gets 'summoned' back up

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'mom's love is like candy hidden in nagging'; rewritten as original composition

zh-tw.content:
我媽的愛從來不是直接說出口的。
它包在「外面很冷多穿一件」裡，藏在「飯煮好了快來吃」裡，躲在「錢夠不夠用」這種明知故問裡。

小時候我只聽到嘮叨，長大才聽懂，那每一句都是在說我愛你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節傳給媽媽的告白型文案，前面鋪陳日常嘮叨，最後一句反轉戳中淚點。配老照片或手寫卡片效果最好
variations: 我花了二十幾年，才把媽媽的嘮叨翻譯成它真正的意思：我愛你、我擔心你、你要好好的
媽媽的『多喝水』『早點睡』『路上小心』，其實都是同一句話的不同說法

en.content:
My mom never says 'I love you' out loud.
It's wrapped inside 'it's cold out, wear another layer,' tucked into 'dinner's ready, come eat,' hidden in 'do you have enough money' even when she already knows.

As a kid I only heard the nagging. It took growing up to realize every single line was just another way of saying I love you.
en.editorNote: A confession-style piece for Mother's Day — everyday nagging builds up, then the last line lands the tears. Best with an old photo or a handwritten card
variations: It took me twenty-some years to translate my mom's nagging into what it actually meant: I love you, I worry about you, please be okay
'Drink more water,' 'go to bed earlier,' 'be careful out there' — they're all the same sentence in different words

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'silence with kids is suspicious' parenting humor; rewritten as original bit

zh-tw.content:
別人家安靜代表歲月靜好。
我媽家安靜代表：有人正在做某件三秒後會讓她尖叫的事。

當媽以後她的聽力進化了，太吵會警覺，太安靜更警覺，只有剛剛好的吵她才敢喘口氣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合有小孩的朋友或媽媽群組，共鳴爆表。母親節傳給媽媽她會邊笑邊說『就是這樣，被你們訓練出來的』
variations: 我媽最怕的不是吵，是突然的安靜，因為那通常代表災難正在醞釀
在我家，安靜不是黃金，是即將爆炸的倒數計時

en.content:
In other houses, silence means peace.
In my mom's house, silence means someone is doing something that will make her scream in about three seconds.

Since becoming a mom her hearing evolved: too loud and she's on alert, too quiet and she's on higher alert. Only the just-right amount of noise lets her exhale.
en.editorNote: Perfect for friends with kids or a mom group — instant recognition. Send it to your mom and she'll laugh and say 'exactly, you kids trained me into this'
variations: My mom's biggest fear isn't noise — it's sudden silence, because that usually means disaster is brewing
In my house, silence isn't golden. It's a countdown timer about to go off

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'when little, wanted to grow up fast; grown up, wish you'd age slowly'; rewritten as original composition

zh-tw.content:
小時候我每天都希望自己快點長大。
現在我每天都希望媽媽慢一點變老。

我們好像在拉同一條時間，只是方向相反。
所以拜託了，媽，能不能走慢一點，多陪我一陣子就好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 母親節走心款文案，適合貼文配老照片，或寫進卡片。前後對照的句式特別容易戳中眼眶
variations: 從前我急著長大，現在我捨不得你變老，原來成長就是學會珍惜倒數的時間
我願意用我長大的那些年，換你慢慢變老的那些年

en.content:
When I was little, I wished every day that I'd grow up faster.
Now I wish every day that Mom would age a little slower.

It's like we're pulling the same thread of time, just in opposite directions.
So please, Mom — could you walk a little slower, and stay with me just a while longer.
en.editorNote: A heartfelt Mother's Day piece, great for a post with an old photo or written into a card. The before-and-after structure is what gets the eyes watery
variations: I used to rush to grow up; now I dread you growing old. Turns out growing up means learning to treasure the time that's counting down
I'd trade all the years I spent growing up for all the years you'll spend growing old slowly

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'mom's superpower is finding things right after you said you looked'; rewritten as original composition

zh-tw.content:
我找東西找了半小時，跟我媽說我家根本沒有。
她走過來，看都沒看就直接從同一個抽屜把它拿出來。
我懷疑那東西是怕她，不是怕我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 幾乎每個人都中過這招，傳到家庭群組保證一片「真的欸」。配上翻箱倒櫃的崩潰表情更好笑
variations: 我說找不到，是給我媽一個展現神力的機會
東西在我手上會消失，在我媽手上會自己現身

en.content:
I searched for half an hour and told my mom the thing simply doesn't exist in this house.
She walked over, didn't even look, and pulled it out of the exact drawer I just checked.
I'm convinced the object is scared of her, not me.
en.editorNote: Almost everyone has lived this exact moment — drop it in the family chat for instant 'so true' replies. Pairs great with a frazzled searching face
variations: Saying 'I can't find it' is just my way of giving Mom a chance to show off her powers
Things vanish in my hands and magically reappear in my mom's

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'sometimes I open my mouth and my mother comes out'; rewritten as original composition

zh-tw.content:
我以前發誓長大絕對不要變成我媽。
結果今天我一開口，跑出來的是「電燈是你家開的喔」。
我沉默了三秒，然後默默把客廳的燈關掉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 獻給所有發現自己活成媽媽翻版的人，傳給朋友或媽媽都很有梗。母親節也適合配一句「謝謝你把我教得這麼像你」
variations: 小時候最受不了的那句話，現在變成我的口頭禪
原來變成媽媽不需要儀式，一句嘮叨就自動升級了

en.content:
I swore I'd never turn into my mom when I grew up.
Then today I opened my mouth and out came 'were you raised in a barn? close the door.'
I went quiet for three seconds, then quietly closed the door.
en.editorNote: For everyone who's caught themselves becoming a carbon copy of their mom. Works as a roast for friends or a sweet 'thanks for the upgrade' for Mother's Day
variations: The line I hated most as a kid is now my personal catchphrase
Turns out becoming your mother needs no ceremony — one nag and the upgrade installs itself

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'mom life: reheated coffee and unfinished sentences'; rewritten as original composition

zh-tw.content:
當媽之後的日常：
一杯重新加熱第四次的咖啡、永遠講到一半就斷掉的句子、還有一座洗了又長出來的衣服山。
我不是很累，我只是被愛榨乾了而已。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合當媽媽的社群貼文文案，配亂亂的桌面或洗衣籃照片超有共鳴。新手爸媽群組轉發率很高
variations: 我的超能力是同時做五件事，再把五件都忘記
靠著愛、咖啡因和一點點混亂撐起整個家

en.content:
Mom life, daily edition:
one coffee reheated for the fourth time, sentences that never quite reach the end, and a laundry mountain that grows back the moment you fold it.
I'm not tired. I'm just lovingly drained.
en.editorNote: A ready-made caption for a mom's social post — pair it with a messy counter or laundry basket photo. Spreads fast in new-parent groups
variations: My superpower is doing five things at once and forgetting all five
Running this whole house on love, caffeine, and a healthy dose of chaos

items.sourceNote: inspired by 'everything you said makes sense now, I hate that / you were right and I wasn't ready to hear it'; rewritten as original composition

zh-tw.content:
媽，妳以前說的那些話，我現在全部都懂了。
最氣的是，妳每一句都是對的。
我只是當年還沒準備好聽進去而已。
所以這次換我說：妳辛苦了，謝謝妳一直等我長大。
zh-tw.editorNote: 走心又帶點幽默的母親節文案，前面吐槽後面收尾，貼進卡片或貼文都很打動人。對話感讓它讀起來像真的在跟媽媽說話
variations: 妳說的我當時左耳進右耳出，現在每一句都迴盪在腦海
長大就是慢慢承認媽媽當年都是對的

en.content:
Mom, all those things you used to say — I finally get every one of them.
The most annoying part is that you were right about all of it.
I just wasn't ready to hear it back then.
So this time let me say it: thank you for working so hard, and for waiting while I grew up.
en.editorNote: A heartfelt-but-cheeky Mother's Day piece — opens with a roast, lands on gratitude. Reads like a real conversation, perfect for a card or post
variations: Everything you said went in one ear and out the other — now it all echoes in my head
Growing up is just slowly admitting Mom was right the whole time
